traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Religion

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A holy symbol (a gift to you when you entered the priesthood), a prayer book or prayer wheel, 5 sticks of incense, vestments, a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Shelter of the Faithful
traits.description:
As an acolyte, you command the respect of those who share your faith, and you can perform the religious ceremonies of your deity. You and your adventuring companions can expect to receive free healing and care at a temple, shrine, or other established presence of your faith, though you must provide any material components needed for spells. Those who share your religion will support you (but only you) at a modest lifestyle.
You might also have ties to a specific temple dedicated to your chosen deity or pantheon, and you have a residence there. This could be the temple where you used to serve, if you remain on good terms with it, or a temple where you have found a new home. While near your temple, you can call upon the priests for assistance, provided the assistance you ask for is not hazardous and you remain in good standing with your temple.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Sleight of Hand

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Disguise Kit, Forgery Kit

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of fine clothes, a disguise kit, tools of the con of your choice (ten stoppered bottles filled with colored liquid, a set of weighted dice, a deck of marked cards, or a signet ring of an imaginary duke), and a belt pouch containing 15 gp.

traits.name: Feature: False Identity
traits.description: You have created a second identity that includes documentation, established acquaintances, and disguises that allow you to assume that persona. Additionally, you can forge documents including official papers and personal letters, as long as you have seen an example of the kind of document or the handwriting you are trying to copy.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Insight

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A uniform in the style of your unit and indicative of your rank, a horn with which to summon help, a set of manacles, and a pouch containing 10 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Watcher's Eye
traits.description:
Your experience in enforcing the law, and dealing with lawbreakers, gives you a feel for local laws and criminals. You can easily find the local outpost of the watch or a similar organization, and just as easily pick out the dens of criminal activity in a community, although you're more likely to be welcome in the former locations rather than the latter.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 145

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: History, Insight

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of artisan's tools

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Dwarvish or one of your choice if you already speak Dwarvish

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of artisan's tools with which you are proficient, a maker's mark chisel used to mark your handiwork with the symbol of the clan of crafters you learned your skill from, a set of traveler's clothes, and a pouch containing 5 gp and a gem worth 10 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Respect of the Stout Folk
traits.description:
As well respected as clan crafters are among outsiders, no one esteems them quite so highly as dwarves do. You always have free room and board in any place where shield dwarves or gold dwarves dwell, and the individuals in such a settlement might vie among themselves to determine who can offer you (and possibly your companions) the finest accomodations and assistance.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 145

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: History, plus your choice of one from among Arcana, Nature, and Religion

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Languages: any two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: The scholar's robes of your cloister, a writing kit (small pouch with a quill, ink, folded parchment, and a small penknife), a borrowed book on the subject of your current study, and a pouch containing 10 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Library Access
traits.description:
Though others must often endure extensive interviews and significant fees to gain access to even the most common archives in your library, you have free and easy access to the majority of the library,, though it might also have repositories of lore that are too valuable, magical, or secret to permit anyone immediate access.
You have a working knowledge of your cloister's personnel and bureaucracy, and you know how to navigate those connections with some ease.
Additionally, you are likely to gain preferential treatment at other libraries across the Realms, as professional courtesy to a fellow scholar.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 146

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Persuasion

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of fine clothes and a pouch containing 5 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Court Functionary
traits.description:
Your knowledge of how bureaucracies function lets you gain access to the records and inner workings of any noble court or government you encounter. You know who the movers and shakers are, whom to go to for the favors you seek, and what the current intrigues of interest in the group are.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 146

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set, thieves' tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A crowbar, a set of dark common clothes including a hood, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Criminal Contact
traits.description: You have a reliable and trustworthy contact who acts as your liaison to a network of other criminals. You know how to get messages to and from your contact, even over great distances; specifically, you know the local messengers, corrupt caravan masters, and seedy sailors who can deliver messages for you.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Acrobatics, Performance

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Disguise kit, one type of musical instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A musical instrument (one of your choice), the favor of an admirer (love letter, lock of hair, or trinket), a costume, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: By Popular Demand
traits.description: You can always find a place to perform, usually in an inn or tavern but possibly with a circus, at a theater, or even in a noble's court. At such a place, you receive free lodging and food of a modest or comfortable standard (depending on the quality of the establishment), as long as you perform each night. In addition, your performance makes you something of a local figure. When strangers recognize you in a town where you have performed, they typically take a liking to you.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight and one Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma skill of your choice, as appropriate to your faction

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Badge or emblem of your faction, a copy of a seminal faction text (or code-book for a covert faction), a set of common clothes, and a pouch containing 15 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Safe Haven
traits.description:
As a faction agent, you have access to a secret network of supporters and operatives who can provide assistance on your adventures. You know a set of secret signs and passwords you can use to identify such operatives, who can provide you with access to a hidden safe house, free room and board, or assistance in finding information. These agents never risk their lives for you or risk revealing their true identities.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 147

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Perception

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Any one musical instrument or gaming set of your choice, likely something native to your homeland

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any one of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: One set of traveler's clothes, any one musical instrument or gaming set you are proficient with, poorly wrought maps from your homeland that depict where you are in Faerûn, a small piece of jewelry worth 10 gp in the style of your homeland's craftsmanship, and a pouch containing 5 gp.

traits.name: Feature: All Eyes on You
traits.description:
Your accent, mannerisms, figures of speech, and perhaps even your appearance all mark you as foreign. Curious glances are directed your way wherever you go, which can be a nuisance, but you also gain the friendly interest of scholars and others intrigued by far-off lands, to say nothing of everyday folk who are eager to hear stories of your homeland.
You can parley this attention into access to people and places you might not otherwise have, for you and your traveling companions. Noble lords, scholars, and merchant princes, to name a few, might be interested in hearing about your distant homeland and people.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 148

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Animal Handling, Survival

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of artisan's tools, vehicles (land)

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of artisan's tools (one of your choice), a shovel, an iron pot, a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Rustic Hospitality
traits.description: Since you come from the ranks of the common folk, you fit in among them with ease. You can find a place to hide, rest, or recuperate among other commoners, unless you have shown yourself to be a danger to them. They will shield you from the law or anyone else searching for you, though they will not risk their lives for you.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of artisan's tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of artisan's tools (one of your choice), a letter of introduction from your guild, a set of traveler's clothes, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Guild Membership
traits.description:
As an established and respected member of a guild, you can rely on certain benefits that membership provides. Your fellow guild members will provide you with lodging and food if necessary, and pay for your funeral if needed. In some cities and towns, a guildhall offers a central place to meet other members of your profession, which can be a good place to meet potential patrons, allies, or hirelings.
Guilds often wield tremendous political power. If you are accused of a crime, your guild will support you if a good case can be made for your innocence or the crime is justifiable. You can also gain access to powerful political figures through the guild, if you are a member in good standing. Such connections might require the donation of money or magic items to the guild's coffers.
You must pay dues of 5 gp per month to the guild. If you miss payments, you must make up back dues to remain in the guild's good graces.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Medicine, Religion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Herbalism Kit

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A scroll case stuffed full of notes from your studies or prayers, a winter blanket, a set of common clothes, an herbalism kit, and 5 gp

traits.name: Feature: Discovery
traits.description:
The quiet seclusion of your extended hermitage gave you access to a unique and powerful discovery. The exact nature of this revelation depends on the nature of your seclusion. It might be a great truth about the cosmos, the deities, the powerful beings of the outer planes, or the forces of nature. It could be a site that no one else has ever seen. You might have uncovered a fact that has long been forgotten, or unearthed some relic of the past that could rewrite history. It might be information that would be damaging to the people who or consigned you to exile, and hence the reason for your return to society.
Work with your DM to determine the details of your discovery and its impact on the campaign.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Survival, plus one from among Arcana, History, and Religion

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any one of your choice

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Your choice of a gaming set or a musical instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Your inheritance, a set of traveler's clothes, any items with which you are proficient, and a pouch containing 15 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Inheritance
traits.description:
Choose or randomly determine your inheritance from the possibilities in the table below. Work with your Dungeon Master to come up with details: Why is your inheritance so important, and what is its full story? You might prefer for the DM to invent these details as part of the game, allowing you to learn more about your inheritance as your character does.
The Dungeon Master is free to use your inheritance as a story hook, sending you on quests to learn more about its history or true nature, or confronting you with foes who want to claim it for themselves or prevent you from learning what you seek. The DM also determines the properties of your inheritance and how they figure into the item's history and importance. For instance, the object might be a minor magic item, or one that begins with a modest ability and increases in potency with the passage of time. Or, the true nature of your inheritance might not be apparent at first and is revealed only when certain conditions are met.
When you begin your adventuring career, you can decide whether to tell your companions about your inheritance right away. Rather than attracting attention to yourself, you might want to keep your inheritance a secret until you learn more about what it means to you and what it can do for you.
d8 - Object or item:
1 - A document such as a map, a letter, or a journal
2-3 - a trinket (see "Trinkets" in chapter 5 of the Player's Handbook)
4 - an article of clothing
5 - a piece of jewelry
6 - an arcane book or formulary
7 - a written story, song, poem, or secret
8 - a tattoo or other body marking
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 150

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Investigation

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A uniform in the style of your unit and indicative of your rank, a horn with which to summon help, a set of manacles, and a pouch containing 10 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Watcher's Eye
traits.description:
Your experience in enforcing the law, and dealing with lawbreakers, gives you a feel for local laws and criminals. You can easily find the local outpost of the watch or a similar organization, and just as easily pick out the dens of criminal activity in a community, although you're more likely to be welcome in the former locations rather than the latter.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 145

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Persuasion, plus one from among Arcana, History, Nature, and Religion, as appropriate for your order

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Your choice of a gaming set or a musical instrument

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any one of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: One set of traveler's clothes, a signet, banner, or seal representing your place or rank in the order, and a pouch containing 10 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Knightly Regard
traits.description:
Your receive shelter and succor from members of your knightly order and those who are sympathetic to its aims. If your order is a religious one, you can gain aid from temples and other religious communities of your deity. Knights of civic orders can get help from the community — whether a lone settlement or a great nation — that they serve, and knights of philosophical orders can find help from those they have aided in pursuit of their ideals, and those who share their ideals.
This help comes in the form of shelter and meals, and healing when appropriate, as well as occasionally risky assistance, such as a band of local citizens rallying to aid a sorely pressed knight, or those who support the order helping to smuggle a knight out of town when he or she is being hunted unjustly.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 151

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set, vehicles (land)

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A uniform of your company (traveler's clothes in quality), an insignia of your rank, a gaming set of your choice, and a pouch containing the remainder of your last wages (10 gp).

traits.name: Feature: Mercenary Life
traits.description:
You know the mercenary life as only someone who has experienced it can. You are able to identify mercenary companies by their emblems, and you know a little about any such company, including who has hired them recently. You can find the taverns and festhalls where mercenaries abide in any area, as long as you speak the language. You can find mercenary work between adventures sufficient to maintain a comfortable lifestyle (see "Practicing a Profession" under "Downtime Activities" in chapter 8 of the Player's Handbook).
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 152

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: History, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of fine clothes, a signet ring, a scroll of pedigree, and a purse containing 25 gp

traits.name: Feature: Position of Privilege
traits.description: Thanks to your noble birth, people are inclined to think the best of you. You are welcome in high society, and people assume you have the right to be wherever you are. The common folk make every effort to accommodate you and avoid your displeasure, and other people of high birth treat you as a member of the same social sphere. You can secure an audience with a local noble if you need to.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Survival

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of musical instrument

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A staff, a hunting trap, a trophy from an animal you killed, a set of traveler's clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Wanderer
traits.description: You have an excellent memory for maps and geography, and you can always recall the general layout of terrain, settlements, and other features around you. In addition, you can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five other people each day, provided that the land offers berries, small game, water, and so forth.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Arcana, History

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A bottle of black ink, a quill, a small knife, a letter from a dead colleague posing a question you have not yet been able to answer, a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Researcher
traits.description: When you attempt to learn or recall a piece of lore, if you do not know that information, you often know where and from whom you can obtain it. Usually, this information comes from a library, scriptorium, university, or a sage or other learned person or creature, Your DM might rule that the knowledge you seek is secreted away in an almost inaccessible place, or that it simply cannot be found. Unearthing the deepest secrets of the multiverse can require an adventure or even a whole campaign.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Perception

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Navigator's tools, vehicles(water)

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A belaying pin (dub), 50 feet of silk rope, a lucky charm such as a rabbit foot or a small stone with a hole in the center (or you may roll for a random trinket on the Trinkets table in chapter 5), a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Ship's Passage
traits.description: When you need to, you can secure free passage on a sailing ship for yourself and your adventuring companions. You might sail on the ship you served on, or another ship you have good relations with (perhaps one captained by a former crewmate). Because you're calling in a favor, you can't be certain of a schedule or route that will meet your every need. Your Dungeon Master will determine how long it takes to get where you need to go. In return for your free passage, you and your companions are expected to assist the crew during the voyage.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Intimidation

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set, vehicles (land)

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: An insignia of rank, a trophy taken from a fallen enemy (a dagger, broken blade, or piece of a banner), a set of bone dice or deck of cards, a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Military Rank
traits.description: You have a military rank from your career as a soldier. Soldiers loyal to your former military organization still recognize your authority and influence, and they defer to you if they are of a lower rank. You can invoke your rank to exert influence over other soldiers and requisition simple equipment or horses for temporary use. You can also usually gain access to friendly military encampments and fortresses where your rank is recognized.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Choose two from among Deception, Insight, Persuasion, and Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Choose two from among one type of gaming set, one musical instrument, and thieves's tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of clothes appropriate to your duties and a pouch containing 20 gp

traits.name: Feature: Ear to the Ground
traits.description:
You are in frequent contact with people in the segment of society that your chosen quarries move through. These people might be associated with the criminal underworld, the rough-and-tumble folk of the streets, or members of high society. This connection comes in the form of a contact in any city you visit, a person who provides information about the people and places of the local area.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 153

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Sleight of Hand, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Disguise Kit, thieves' tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A small knife, a map of the city you grew up in, a pet mouse, a token to remember your parents by, a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: City Secrets
traits.description: You know the secret patterns and flow to cities and can find passages through the urban sprawl that others would miss. When you are not in combat, you (and companions you lead) can travel between any two locations in the city twice as fast as your speed would normally allow.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Survival

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any one of your choice

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of musical instrument or artisan's tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A hunting trap, a totemic token or set of tattoos marking your loyalty to Uthgar and your tribal totem, a set of traveler's clothes, and a pouch containing 10 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Uthgardt Heritage
traits.description:
You have an excellent knowledge of not only your tribe's territory, but also the terrain and natural resources of the rest of the North. You are familiar enough with any wilderness area that you can find twice as much food and water as you normally would when you forage there.
Additionally, you can call upon the hospitality of your people, and those allied with  your tribe, often including members of the druid circles, tribes of nomadic elves, the Harpers, and the priesthoods devoted to the gods of the First Circle.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 153

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: History, Persuasion

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Any one of your choice

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set or one musical instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of fine clothes, a signet ring or brooch, a scroll of pedigree, a skin of fine zzar or wine, and a purse containing 20 gp.

traits.name: Feature: Kept in Style
traits.description:
While you are in Waterdeep or elsewhere in the North, your house sees to your everyday needs. Your name and signet are sufficient to cover most of your expenses; the inns, taverns, and festhalls you frequent are glad to record your debt and send an accounting to your family's estate in Waterdeep to settle what you owe.
This advantage enables you to live a comfortable lifestyle without having to pay 2 gp a day for it, or reduces the cost of a wealthy or aristocratic lifestyle by that amount. You may not maintain a less affluent lifestyle and use the difference as income — the benefit is a line of credit, not an actual monetary reward.
Source: Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide, page 154

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set, thief's tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A crowbar, a set of dark common clothes including a hood, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Spy Contact
traits.description: You have a reliable and trustworthy contact who acts as your liaison to a network of other spies. You know how to get messages to and from your contact, even over great distances; specifically, you know the local messengers, corrupt caravan masters, and seedy sailors who can deliver messages for you.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Acrobatics, Performance

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Disguise kit, one type of musical instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: An inexpensive but unusual weapon, such as a trident or net (one of your choice), the favor of an admirer (love letter, lock of hair, or trinket), a costume, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: By Popular Demand
traits.description: You can find a place to perform in any place that features combat for entertainment-perhaps a gladiatorial arena or secret pit fighting club. In such an area, you receive free lodging and food of a modest or comfortable standard (depending on the quality of the establishment), as long as you perform each night. In addition, your performance makes you something of a local figure. When strangers recognize you in a town where you have performed, they typically take a liking to you.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Navigator's Tools or an additional language

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A mule and cart, a letter of introduction from your guild, a set of traveler's clothes, and a belt pouch containing 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Guild Membership
traits.description:
As an established and respected member of a guild or large merchant consortium, you can rely on certain benefits that membership provides. Your fellow guild members will provide you with lodging and food if necessary, and pay for your funeral if needed. In some cities and towns, a guildhall offers a central place to meet other members of your profession, which can be a good place to meet potential patrons, allies, or hirelings.
Guilds or consortiums often wield tremendous political power. If you are accused of a crime, your guild will support you if a good case can be made for your innocence or the crime is justifiable. You can also gain access to powerful political figures through the guild, if you are a member in good standing. Such connections might require the donation of money or magic items to the guild's coffers.
You must pay dues of 5 gp per month to the guild. If you miss payments, you must make up back dues to remain in the guild's good graces.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: History, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A banner or other token from a noble lord or lady to whom you have given your heart, a set of fine clothes, a signet ring, a scroll of pedigree, and a purse containing 25 gp

traits.name: Feature: Retainers
traits.description:
You have the service of three retainers loyal to your family. These retainers can be attendants or messengers, and one might be a majordomo. One of your retainers is a noble who serves as your squire, aiding you in exchange for training on his or her own path to knighthood. Your two remaining retainers might include a groom to care for your horse and a servant who polishes your armor (and even helps you put it on).
Your retainers (2 commoners, 1 noble) can perform mundane tasks for you, but they do not fight for you, will not follow you into obviously dangerous areas (such as dungeons), and will leave if they are frequently endangered or abused.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Perception

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Navigator's tools, vehicles(water)

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A belaying pin (dub), 50 feet of silk rope, a lucky charm such as a rabbit foot or a small stone with a hole in the center (or you may roll for a random trinket on the Trinkets table in chapter 5), a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Bad Reputation
traits.description: No matter where you go, people are afraid of you due to your reputation. When you are in a civilized settlement, you can get away with minor criminal offenses, such as refusing to pay for food at a tavern or breaking down doors at a local shop, since most people will not report your activity to the authorities.

traits.name: Feature: Harrowing Event
traits.description:
Prior to becoming an adventurer, your path in life was defined by one dark moment, one fateful decision, or one tragedy. Now you feel a darkness threatening to consume you, and you fear there may be no hope of escape. Choose a harrowing event that haunts you, or roll 1d10.
1. A monster that slaughtered dozens of innocent people spared your life, and you don't know why.
2. You were born under a dark star. You can feel it watching you, coldly and distantly. Sometimes it beckons you in the dead of night.
3. An apparition that has haunted your family for generations now haunts you. You don't know what it wants, and it won't leave you alone.
4. Your family has a history of practicing the dark arts. You dabbled once and felt something horrible clutch at your soul, whereupon you  ed in terror.
5. An oni took your sibling one cold, dark night, and you were unable to stop it.
6. You were cursed with lycanthropy and later cured. You are now haunted by the innocents you slaughtered.
7. A hag kidnapped and raised you. You escaped, but the hag still has a magical hold over you and fills your mind with evil thoughts.
8. You opened an eldritch tome and saw things un t for a sane mind. You burned the book, but its words and images are burned into your psyche.
9. A fiend possessed you as a child. You were locked away but escaped. The fiend is still inside you, but now you try to keep it locked away.
10. You did terrible things to avenge the murder of someone you loved. You became a monster, and it haunts your waking dreams.

traits.name: Personality Trait
traits.description:
Choose or randomly determine
1. I don't run from evil. Evil runs from me.
2. I like to read and memorize poetry. It keeps me calm and brings me fleeting moments of happiness.
3. I spend money freely and live life to the fullest, knowing that tomorrow I might die.
4. I live for the thrill of the hunt.
5. I don't talk about the thing that torments me. I'd rather not burden others with my curse.
6. I expect danger around every corner.
7. I refuse to become a victim, and I will not allow others to be victimized.
8. I put no trust in divine beings.

traits.name: Ideal
traits.description:
Choose or randomly determine
1. I try to help those in need, no matter what the personal cost. (Good)
2. I'll stop the spirits that haunt me or die trying. (Any)
3. I kill monsters to make the world a safer place, and to exorcise my own demons. (Good)
4. I have a dark calling that puts me above the law. (Chaotic)
5. I like to know my enemy's capabilities and weaknesses before rushing into battle. (Lawful)
6. I'm a monster that destroys other monsters, and anything else that gets in my way. (Evil)

traits.name: Bond
traits.description:
Choose or randomly determine
1. I keep my thoughts and discoveries in a journal. My journal is my legacy.
2. I would sacrifice my life and my soul to protect the innocent.
3. My torment drove away the person I love. I strive to win back the love I've lost.
4. A terrible guilt consumes me. I hope that I can find redemption through my actions.
5. There's evil in me, I can feel it. It must never be set free.
6. I have a child to protect. I must make the world a safer place for him (or her).

traits.name: Flaw
traits.description:
Choose or randomly determine
1. I have certain rituals that I must follow every day. I can never break them.
2. I assume the worst in people.
3. I feel no compassion for the dead. They're the lucky ones.
4. I have an addiction.
5. I am a purveyor of doom and gloom who lives in a world without hope.
6. I talk to spirits that no one else can see.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Chose two of Arcana, Investigation, Religion, or Survival

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Choose one exotic language (Abyssal, Celestial, Deep Speech, Draconic, Infernal, Primordial, Sylvan, or Undercommon)

traits.name: Feature: Heart of Darkness
traits.description: Those who look into your eyes can see that you have faced unimaginable horror and that you are no stranger to darkness. Though they might fear you, commoners will extend you every courtesy and do their utmost to help you. Unless you have shown yourself to be a danger to them, they will even take up arms to fight alongside you, should you find yourself facing an enemy alone.

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Monster hunter's pack, one trinket of special significance (choose one or roll on the Gothic Trinkets table)

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Nature, Survival

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of artisan's tools

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Elven

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A two-person tent, artisan's tools, a holy symbol, a set of traveler's clothes, a belt pouch, 5 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Poor

traits.name: Feature: Shelter of the Elven Clergy
traits.description:
The clerics of Elventree have vowed to care for the Cormanthor refugees. They will help you when they can, including providing you and your companions with free healing and care at temples, shrines, and other established presences in Elventree. They will also provide you (but only you) with a poor lifestyle.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Acolyte feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Survival

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Thieves' tools, one type of musical instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A battered alms box, a musical instrument, a cast-off military jacket, cap, or scarf, a set of common clothes, a belt pouch, and 10 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Poor

traits.name: Feature: Red Plume and Mage Guild Contacts
traits.description:
You made a number of friends among the Red Plumes and the Mage's Guild when you lived at the Hillsfar Gate. They remember you fondly and help you in little ways when they can. You can invoke their assistance in and around Hillsfar to obtain food, as well as simple equipment for temporary use. You can also invoke it to gain access to the low-security areas of their garrisons, halls, and encampments.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Soldier feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Land vehicles and water vehicles

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A fine set of clothes, a signet ring, a letter of introduction from your family's trading house, and a purse containing 25 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Wealthy

traits.name: Feature: Factor
traits.description:
Although you’ve left the day-to-day life of a merchant behind, your family has assigned you the services of a loyal retainer from the business, a factor, husbanding agent, seafarer, caravan guard, or clerk. This individual is a commoner who can perform mundane tasks for you such as making purchases, delivering messages, and running errands. He or she will not fight for you and will not follow you into obviously dangerous areas (such as dungeons), and will leave if frequently endangered or abused. If he or she is killed, the family assigns you another within a few days.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Noble Knight feature.]

traits.name: Alternate Feature: Trade Contact
traits.description:
You and your family have trade contacts such as caravan masters, shopkeepers, sailors, artisans, and farmers throughout the Moonsea region and all along the Sword Coast. Once per game session, when adventuring in either of those areas, you can use those contacts to get information about the local area or to pass a message to someone in those areas, even across the great distance between the two areas.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Criminal Contact and Researcher feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Perception, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Forgery kit

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One racial language

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A forgery kit, a set of common clothes, a belt pouch, and 5 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Modest

traits.name: Feature: Secret Passage
traits.description:
You can call on your contacts within the smuggling community to secure secret passage into or out of Hillsfar for yourself and your adventuring companions, no questions asked, and no Red Plume entanglements. Because you’re calling in a favor, you can’t be certain they will be able to help on your timetable or at all. Your Dungeon Master will determine whether you can be smuggled into or out of the city. In return for your passage, you and your companions may owe the Rouges Guild a favor and/or may have to pay bribes.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Sailor feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Disguise kit, forgery kit

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A disguise kit, a forgery kit, a set of common clothes, a belt pouch, 5 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Modest

traits.name: Feature: Secret Identity
traits.description:
You have created a secret identity that you use to conceal your true race and that offers a covering explanation for your presence in Hillsfar. In addition, you can forge documents, including official papers and personal letters, as long as you have seen an example of the kind of document or the handwriting you are trying to copy.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Charlatan feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Intimidation

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Forgery kit

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Netherese

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A forgery kit, a transparent cylinder of shadow that has no opening, a signet ring, a set of fine clothes, and 15 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Moderate

traits.name: Feature: Secret Society
traits.description:
You have a special way of communicating with others who feel the same way you do about the Shade. When you enter a village or larger city you can identify contact who will give you information on those that would hinder your goals and those would help you simply because of your desire to see the Shade Enclave return in all its glory.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Criminal feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Investigation, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Thieves kit

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Elven

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A thieves kit, a gray cloak, Sherrif's insignia (badge), a set of fine clothes, and 17 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Moderate

traits.name: Feature: Investigative Services
traits.description:
You are part of a small force outside of Hillsfar You have a special way of communicating with others and they seem to be at ease around you. You can invoke your rank to allow you access to a crime scene or to requisition equipment or horses on a temporary basis. When you enter a town or village around Hillsfar you can identify a contact who will give you information on the local rumors and would help you simply because of your desire to get answers and information for anyone wanting to disrupt trade.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Soldier feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Animal Handling, Survival

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: Land vehicles

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A whip, a tent, a regional map, a set of traveling clothes, and a belt pouch containing 10 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Poor

traits.name: Feature: Wagonmaster
traits.description:
You are used to being in charge of the operation and your reputation for reliability has you on a short list when the job is critical. Experience has taught you to rely on your gut. Others recognize this and look to you for direction when a situation gets serious. You are able to identify the most defensible locations for camping. If you are part of a caravan outfit, you are able to attract two additional workers that are loyal to you based on your reputation. You have an excellent memory for maps and geography and can always determine your cardinal directions while traveling.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Outlander feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Survival

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: Dwarven and Undercommon

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A shovel or a miner's pick, a block and tackle, a climber's kit, a set of common clothes, and a belt pouch containing 5 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Poor

traits.name: Feature: Deep Miner
traits.description:
You are used to navigating the deep places of the earth. You never get lost in caves or mines if you have either seen an accurate map of them or have been through them before. Furthermore, you are able to scrounge fresh water and food for yourself and as many as five other people each day if you are in a mine or natural caves.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Outlander feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Sleight of Hand

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of gaming set, water vehicles

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Fishing tackle, dice set, playing card set, or Three Dragon Ante set, a set of common clothes, rowboat, and a belt containing 5 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Poor

traits.name: Feature: Harborfolk
traits.description:
You grew up on the docks and waters of Mulmaster Harbor. The harborfolk remember you and still treat you as one of them. They welcome you and your companions. While they might charge you for it, they'll always offer what food and shelter they have; they'll even hide you if the City Watch is after you (but not if the Hawks are).
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Folk Hero feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Performance

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of artistic artisan's tools and one musical instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: One set of artisan's tools or musical instrument, a set of fine clothes, and a purse containing 10 gp

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Wealthy

traits.name: Feature: Highborn
traits.description:
Mulmaster is run by and for its aristoracy. Every other class of citizen in the city defers to you, and even the priesthood, Soldiery, Hawks, and Cloaks treat you with deference. Other aristocrats and nobles accept you in their circles and likely know you or of you. Your connections can get you the ear of a Zor or Zora under the right circumstances.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Noble feature.]

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Athletics

traits.name: Tool Proficiencies
traits.description: One type of artisan's tools

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A set of artisan’s tools (one of your choice), a token of the life you once knew, a set of traveler’s clothes, and a belt pouch containing 15 gps

traits.name: Starting Lifestyle
traits.description: Modest

traits.name: Feature: Phlan Survivor
traits.description:
Whatever your prior standing was, you are now one of the many refugees that have come to Mulmaster. You are able to find refuge with others from Phlan and those who sympathize with your plight. Within Mulmaster this means that you can find a place to bed down, recover, and hide from the watch with either other refugees from Phlan, or the Zhents within the ghettos.
[Note: This feature is a variant of the Folk Hero feature.]

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1 (choose when you gain this background).

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Religion

traits.description: Calligrapher's Supplies

traits.name: Language
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Calligrapher's supplies, a book (prayers), a holy symbol, a set of common clothes, and 50 gp

traits.name: Feature: Shelter of the Faithful
traits.description: You command respect from those who share your faith. You can perform religious ceremonies, and you and your companions can receive free healing and care at temples of your faith (when material components are provided).

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Dexterity, or Intelligence by 1.

traits.description: Investigation, Persuasion

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Artisan's tools (same type as proficiency), a letter of introduction from your guild, a set of traveler's clothes, and 40 gp

traits.name: Feature: Guild Membership
traits.description: Your guild supports you as long as you maintain membership. In a guild town, you can rely on member support for lodging, food, and local information.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Charisma or Dexterity by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Deception, Sleight of Hand

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Forgery Kit

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Forgery kit, a set of fine clothes, a trinket representing your con specialty, and 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: False Identity
traits.description: You have a false identity with documentation and established acquaintances. You can forge documents as long as you have seen an example.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Dexterity or Intelligence by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Sleight of Hand, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Thieves' Tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Thieves' tools, a crowbar, a set of dark common clothes with a hood, and 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Criminal Contact
traits.description: You have a reliable and trustworthy contact who acts as your liaison to a network of criminals.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Dexterity, or Charisma by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Acrobatics, Performance

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: One type of Musical Instrument

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Musical instrument (same type as proficiency), a favor from an admirer, a costume, and 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: By Popular Demand
traits.description: You can always find a place to perform. In exchange for a nightly performance, you receive lodging and food. Performing makes you a local celebrity.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Constitution, or Wisdom by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Animal Handling, Nature

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Carpenter's Tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Carpenter's tools, a shovel, an iron pot, a set of traveler's clothes, and 30 gp

traits.name: Feature: Farm Life
traits.description: You have the experience of a rural upbringing. You can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five other people each day, as long as the land offers natural resources.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Intelligence, or Wisdom by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Perception

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: One type of Gaming Set

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A gaming set (same type as proficiency), a horn, a set of traveler's clothes, and 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Watcher's Eye
traits.description: Your experience with enforcement gives you familiarity with local laws and criminal activity. You can easily find the local watch or pick out criminal dens.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Dexterity, Constitution, or Wisdom by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Stealth, Survival

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Cartographer's Tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Cartographer's tools, a staff, a hunting trap, a set of traveler's clothes, and 15 gp

traits.name: Feature: Wanderer
traits.description: You have an excellent memory for maps and geography, and you can always recall the general layout of terrain, settlements, and other features around you.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Medicine, Religion

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Herbalism Kit

traits.name: Language
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Herbalism kit, a scroll case with notes, a winter blanket, a set of common clothes, and 5 gp

traits.name: Feature: Discovery
traits.description: The quiet seclusion of your extended hermitage gave you access to a unique discovery — a truth about the cosmos, the gods, the forces of nature, or some other profound secret.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Dexterity, or Charisma by 1.

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Navigator's Tools

traits.name: Language
traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Navigator's tools, a horse-drawn cart, a set of traveler's clothes, and 30 gp

traits.name: Feature: Supply Lines
traits.description: You have reliable contacts in most major towns and cities who can help you locate goods, ships, or people who deal in the types of goods you need.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Intelligence, or Charisma by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: History, Persuasion

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: One type of Gaming Set

traits.description: One of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A gaming set (same type as proficiency), a set of fine clothes, a signet ring, a scroll of pedigree, and 25 gp

traits.name: Feature: Position of Privilege
traits.description: Thanks to your noble birth, people are inclined to think the best of you. You are welcome in high society. Common folk make every effort to accommodate you, and you can secure an audience with local nobles.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Constitution, Intelligence, or Wisdom by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Arcana, History

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Calligrapher's Supplies

traits.name: Language
traits.description: Two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Calligrapher's supplies, a book of lore, a letter from a dead colleague, and 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Researcher
traits.description: When you attempt to learn or recall a piece of lore, if you don't know the information, you often know where and from whom you can obtain it.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Strength, Dexterity, or Wisdom by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Perception

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Navigator's Tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Navigator's tools, a silk rope (50 feet), a lucky charm, a set of traveler's clothes, and 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Ship's Passage
traits.description: When you need to, you can secure free passage on a sailing ship for yourself and your companions. You might sail on the ship you served on, or another. In return, you are expected to assist the crew during the voyage.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Investigation, Perception

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Calligrapher's Supplies

traits.name: Language
traits.description: Two of your choice

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Calligrapher's supplies, a book of lore, a set of traveler's clothes, and 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Reliable Information
traits.description: Your training as a scribe lets you quickly find relevant passages in books and documents. When you research a topic, you can find information twice as fast as a normal person.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Athletics, Intimidation

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: One type of Gaming Set

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: A gaming set (same type as proficiency), a dagger, a set of traveler's clothes, and 10 gp

traits.name: Feature: Military Rank
traits.description: You have a military rank from your career as a soldier. Soldiers loyal to your former military organization still recognize your authority and influence.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Increase Dexterity, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1.

traits.name: Skill Proficiencies
traits.description: Insight, Stealth

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: Thieves' Tools

traits.name: Equipment
traits.description: Thieves' tools, a small chest, a set of common clothes, and 16 gp

traits.name: Feature: Drifter's Intuition
traits.description: You have lived on the road and learned to read people and places quickly. You can always find the local tavern, the safest camping spot, or the most trusted fence.
